title: Artisanal Fisher Livelihoods and Economic Survey
abstract: Cross-sectional socio-economic survey of artisanal fishing households along the Red Sea coast, covering income, gear investment, market access, and perceived climate and regulatory pressures on livelihoods.

variables.name: fisher_household_income
variables.label: Annual Household Income from Fishing
variables.description: Self-reported annual net income attributable to fishing activity.

variables.name: gear_investment
variables.label: Gear and Vessel Investment
variables.description: Estimated current replacement value of owned fishing gear and vessel.

variables.name: market_access_score
variables.label: Market Access Score
variables.description: Composite index of distance to market, price transparency, and cold-chain availability.

access.how_to_request: Available to KAUST-affiliated researchers via DataWaha login; external requests reviewed case-by-case by the steward.

provenance.citation: Bakhsh, O. et al. 2023. Livelihoods of Artisanal Fishers on the Saudi Red Sea Coast. KAUST Working Paper Series.

known_issues: Farasan Islands subsample collected by a different enumerator team; minor survey-instrument variation.
